title: Varronia tarodaea

The classification of Varronia tarodaea reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Boraginaceae family.

intro: Varronia tarodaea, also known as "Cachote" or "Pata de Vaca," is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties.
